{{Infobox location
| name = Tartarus
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Tartarus Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Tartarus is a [[Territory]] that serves as a source of power for specific [[Travelers]]. These individuals, often functioning as disciplined soldiers, are characterized by their use of gleaming [[steel]] and silver keys to summon various objects and entities through [[Gates]]. Their summons include spinning blades, needle sprays, tornadoes of glass, and metallic beasts such as armored bears. The material associated with this [[Territory]] is a nearly unbreakable, mirror-bright [[steel]] used to craft massive weapons like war-hammers and swords. In addition to individual [[Travelers]], Tartarus is linked to powerful summoned entities known as [[Incarnations]]. These manifestations can appear as massive, armored giants with voices like grinding stone. While these [[Incarnations]] possess immense strength and can be reconstructed by certain individuals, they can also be physically combative. [[The Territory]] is also connected to the existence of specific [[Trees]] and has been involved in historical conflicts, including a failed rebellion led by a Grandmaster. Furthermore, Tartarus is one of the forces involved in the chaotic formation of [[Orgrith Cave]].}}

== House of Blades ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Tartarus]] is associated with [[Miram]]'s title as Master [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Tartarus]] is a possible power or path [[Leah]] associates with [[Simon's sword]] summoning.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Tartarus]] is a location where beasts of razor-sharp [[steel]] originate.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c23">''House of Blades'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Tartarus is a silver-key [[Territory]] that still stands today, serving as a location where honor holds no value. It is a place from which low-ranking [[Travelers]] may originate, and it is also the site where specific armor was forged. Beyond being a location, Tartarus refers to a type of [[Traveler]] that guards are capable of summoning. Additionally, the name identifies a group of men and women who wear armored uniforms.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus]] is a place where honor does not matter.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus]] is a type of [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Armor was forged in [[Tartarus]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus]] is a location from which low-ranking [[Travelers]] might originate.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus]] is a silver-key [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus]] is a group of men and women in armored uniforms.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus]] is a type of [[Traveler]] that can be summoned by guards.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus]] is a location that still stands.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|As one of the nine [[Territories]], Tartarus is a Grandmaster who oversees a Labyrinth. This ten-foot metal giant is clad in mirrored [[steel]] armor and possesses blank clockwork eyes made of twisting bronze gears. When speaking, the [[Incarnation]]'s voice resembles grinding rocks, and he wields a solid, shining red [[sword]] marked with twisting black lettering. During combat, he swung his blade at [[Kai]]'s neck, though his team was capable of subduing [[Leah]]. Ultimately, Tartarus was defeated and his pieces were dragged away, leaving behind three women who wear his armor behind [[Grandmaster Naraka]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">13</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] contains a Labyrinth.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] is a Grandmaster.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Three women behind [[Grandmaster Naraka]] wear the armor of [[Tartarus]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Elysian Incarnation]] has recruited thirty-seven [[Tartarus Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] is a metal giant with a helmeted head that uses twisting bronze gears instead of eyes.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13">''City of Light'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] has a voice that sounds like grinding rocks.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] wields a solid, shining red [[sword]] with twisting black lettering on its flat surface.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] has a team that could subdue [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] has been defeated and his pieces are being dragged away.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] is one of the nine [[Territories]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] is a ten-foot giant in mirrored [[steel]] armor.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] is an [[Incarnation]] with blank clockwork eyes.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] swung a [[sword]] at [[Kai]]'s neck.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|One of the servants bowed. “He is personally overseeing the war in Helgard, Highness.” ... both servants were actually low-ranking Travelers, probably from Tartarus or Asphodel.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“I’m sure Tartarus still stands as well,” Indirial added.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
